title: audit-test structured emission (v0.3)
description: The machine-readable batch provenance tally `/audit-test --emit-json` writes for Gate to ingest (the B→A graduation, TzolkinB/skills#49). It carries only the per-class COUNTS — the model's per-test judgment (which test is hollow) crystallised into numbers. Gate DERIVES the category (verdict.result + label) from these counts mechanically, exactly as it restates Playwright's `stats`; the gate downstream reads only the derived category, never these counts (honesty guard #1). Deriving the label at ingest is what makes the theater guard structural: deepAudited=0 derives `unexamined`, which can never reach `ship`.

scope.description: informational — what was audited, e.g. "--changed", a glob, or a file path

deepAudited.description: tests that advanced past triage to a mutation run

confirmedHollow.description: 🔴 confirmed false-confidence (execution-confirmed; mutation survived, reachability confirmed)

likelyHollow.description: 🟡 likely false-confidence (reasoned only — env not runnable / stale harness)

baselineLock.description: ⚠️ baseline-lock suspected (live assertion pinned to a regressed value)

unexamined.description: triaged clean, never mutated — NOT evidence of health

runs.description: OPTIONAL (#140, ADR-0037 B2) — a per-test run trace, one record per test a mutation was actually EXECUTED against. Only the execution-confirmed subset (confirmedSolid + confirmedHollow) gets a record; likelyHollow (env not runnable) and baselineLock tests carry no record and keep their reasoned-only standing — B2 covers only the executed subset, never implies coverage of tests it didn't run. Absent entirely, the emission is unchanged from v0.2. Gate cross-checks this trace against the counts (TzolkinB/skills#142); this schema only shapes it.

mutation.description: what was mutated, e.g. "removed the clashes.length > 0 guard"

outcome.description: killed = confirmedSolid (test failed as it should); survived = confirmedHollow (test stayed green)

exitCode.description: the single-test run's process exit code

$comment: Gate derives: result = confirmedHollow>0 ? FAILED : (likelyHollow>0 || baselineLock>0) ? WARNED : PASSED. label = (confirmedSolid>0 || confirmedHollow>0) ? confirmed : deepAudited>0 ? likely : unexamined. ship-eligible ⟺ result=PASSED AND label=confirmed. NO confidence/score field — its arrival is the signal calibration has landed (schemaVersion bump). INGEST GUARDS (#111): `schema` is matched EXACTLY (a model produced this — a bogus `gate-audit-test/v999` is rejected, not accepted by prefix), and the counts must be internally consistent or the whole emission is rejected (degrades to opaque/absent, never a silent upgrade): audited === deepAudited + unexamined, and confirmedSolid+confirmedHollow+likelyHollow+baselineLock ≤ deepAudited. So `{confirmedSolid:1, deepAudited:0}` can never derive `confirmed`. v0.2 = proven -> confirmed taxonomy rename only, no data-model change (#126, ADR-0034). v0.3 = ADDITIVE optional `runs[]` trace (#140, ADR-0037 B2) — an emission with no `runs[]` remains valid and behaves exactly as v0.2.
